reviews.review_text:
Alright, 100 hours deep into Black Myth: Wukong and I think I'm finally ready to talk about it properly.
First thing first, the combat. The three stances thing sounds gimmicky on paper but after a few chapters you start switching between Smash and Pillar mid-combo without thinking about it. Pillar stance is goofy looking, you literally pole vault and charge a heavy from above, but god does it feel good when you crack a boss's head open with a focus point dump. Thrust stance I barely touched until chapter 4 and now I won't shut up about it. It's the move pool the game refuses to explain properly.
The bosses are where this game lives or dies for most people. Yellow Wind Sage took me maybe six tries and I thought I was hot stuff. Then Tiger Vanguard humbled me into a paste. The real wall though was Yellowbrow, not because he's mechanically the hardest but because he keeps yapping at you between phases and if you die you have to listen to it again. That fight made me put the controller down twice. Erlang is the best fight in the game and probably one of the best boss fights I've played in years. That whole sequence is a love letter to anyone who grew up on wuxia stuff.
Now the stuff that's not so great.
Chapter 4 is where the game kind of loses the plot for a bit. Everyone says it, everyone is right. The level design becomes weirdly mazey, there's a stealth section that nobody asked for, and the boss density drops off. It picks back up by the end but the pacing in that middle stretch is rough.
The camera. Look, I know every action game with big bosses has camera problems, but some of these arena fights legitimately become you fighting the lock-on more than the boss. Kang-Jin Loong, you spend half the time getting clipped by the tail because the camera can't decide what to do with a snake-shaped enemy. I switched to free cam for half the late game bosses.
Story is fine. If you don't know Journey to the West going in you're going to be lost and the game does very little to help you. I had to watch a YouTube explainer between chapter 2 and 3 just to understand who half these characters were. The animated cutscenes between chapters are gorgeous though, every studio that worked on those went hard. Different art style each time, very cool touch.
Performance on PC at launch was rough. I'm running a 4050 and chapter 1 had stutters that drove me crazy until I turned off frame gen. They've patched it a few times since and it's much better now, but if you're on lower end hardware, manage your expectations.
The transformation system is underused. You unlock these cool boss forms and most of them are situationally great but the cooldown is so long you forget you have them. I beat the secret final boss by spamming Cloud Step and the rock form transformation, which felt kind of cheesy but also extremely satisfying.
Replay value is there but it's not Elden Ring level open ended. New Game Plus exists, there's a true ending path that requires backtracking and finding specific items in earlier chapters that you have zero clue about your first run. Without a guide you're not finishing this game 100 percent, full stop. The hidden areas in chapters 2 and 5 are some of the best parts of the game and you can completely miss them.
100 hours later, would I recommend it? Yeah, with caveats. If you're a Souls player coming in expecting Elden Ring with monkeys, you'll be disappointed because this isn't really that. It's more linear, more cinematic, more boss rush. But as its own thing it's confident, beautiful, and the combat has way more depth than the marketing showed. The Chinese mythology stuff is genuinely refreshing after years of Norse and Greek dominating the AAA space.
Game Science cooked. They tripped a few times but they cooked.
9.5 out of 10 from me. Loses points for chapter 4, the camera, and the story being borderline impenetrable for newcomers. Gains them all back for Erlang, the art direction, and the fact that staff combat finally has a worthy modern game.

Black Myth - Wukong isn't something I cant write about. Nothing I can put to words will get across to you what it has felt like to play this game. It's currently 12:10am on the 29/05/2026. I know I very late to the party. But all I can say is that I highly recommend you take the journey to the west.

MASTERPIECE but...
This one did not start very well and over time turned into masterpiece.

Act 1 is fairly linear. There is a clear idea of boss rushing. I have felt that I was fighting more bosses then regular enemies. Overwhelming amount of time is spent fighting bosses. Consequently, there is nothing that will stabilise the situation and let you get the know the place (because of linear map). It would be good if the player was put in a position of exploration and figuring out mechanics FIRST and then slowly introduce boss fights (like in other acts). It slowly started to annoy me. Due to stunningly beautiful graphics and environments I pushed through. This was the only reason that kept me going.

Act 2 brings more "open" space to explore. Also best looking chapter as well. This chapter mellows down on bosses and gives a bit more space to breathe. It is very interesting and stunning looking. Another big checkpoint here is Tiger boss fight. This is the first proper challenge. While still trying to figure out ins and outs of the game this one takes time to beat...and it took. There was a bit of relief but also anxiety of what's more to come.
Continuing this chapter you arrive at THE BOSS. Yellow Wind Sage. I thought TIger was hard HAHAHAHAHA.
This is Malenia level boss. As with all bosses, over time I got to learn fairly well sequence of the attacks, however you will still take damage here and there. Coming into last last part of the battle was very challenging. Then the difficuly just increases as he casts the spell where where already hard attacks turn impossible,
This was the boiling point for me. The game does not provide any gear (apart from the basic starter kit) and keeps getting bosses more difficult. This falls in the category of Elden Rings toughest bosses. Unfortunately, as I don't have so much time to play, getting stuck for days on a single boss is no option for me. After days of short sessions I took a different approach. I was going to explore all of Act2 and then come back for one final try. This was gonna make it or break it. I also resorted to look online for secrets that I missed. Turns out that there are secrets and weapons that are critical if you want to defeat this boss. These are easily missable. Having no gui to guide you for any mission you would need to decrypt poetic nature of the game. Having nothing to lose and with all the secrets completed came back to the boss and still took me some to beat. But it was done!
This is the moment where I realized that actually looking for secrets online and having them completed made it soooo enjoyable!!!! Otherwise, I would have missed this!!!

Armed with secrets I went on to Act 3, Act4, Act 5 Act 6.  I learned more of the mechanic. Got more skill. Got my way of dealing with enemies. All other challenging bosses are nowhere near difficulty of Yellow Wind Sage. This is what this game missed to do. Progression of boss fight and having so many of them might need to be turned down. Yes, it is cool but at the diss satisfaction of the player. I wish Act 1 and Act 2 were redesigned mechanically.

Graphics and level design are very strong areas. In addition, animations, anime and camera work is very engaging.
Storyline is very poetic in nature, apart from basic trip to the West best (new) wukong I got nothing.
For better understanding familiarity with Chinese culture is essential.

Overall, I loved it for stunning looks and great mechanics (once discovered). From Act3 onwards it just kept growing on me.

The game is really enjoyable however there is some items that if you find would greatly change how to fight certain bosses and to backtrack to get those certain items requires a video and the items name your looking for. you might not even know about it till your getting your ass beat and look up the reason why. then you will be like damn im not even sure how to get that or where lol

To anyone who feels soulslike's are not your cup of tea, haven't tried any and do not intend to - this game could be the perfect gateway to the genre; without the jank and a lenient, more conservative learning curve. I went into this expecting a brutal challenge and came out pleasantly surprised at how balanced and enjoyable ALL of the boss fights were. There was no fight that took me more than an hour to beat and when I did, the satisfaction was immense. 

Game is absolutely gorgeous, the various tools at your disposal make the Sun Wukong power fantasy extremely satisfying and the visuals, boss designs are on point. My only issues with the game were its exploration, which often was unintuitive and made things VERY easy to miss. Also to anyone unfamiliar with the whole Journey to the West storyline, some lore aspects will require further reading and looking up. Overall, 10/10 and should have won GOTY no questions asked.

I was excited to play this, but the combat/movement feels a bit clunky and it ruins it for me.
The cinematics and lore are very good, but were not enough to keep the game enjoyable.

Just bought the game yesterday and played it for 3 hours. This is one of the best gameplays from the start, the introduction scene is one of the best i ever seen and the graphic is amazing! All day ive been thinking about finish work and get back to play and explore more of this masterpiece.
Such an amazing game. I am hoping the next game from the developer hold up to this quality. I definitely will play again. Once I get through some of my backlog.

it's a good game, good graphics, good story and gameplay. I had some issues with shaders and ray tracing but it wasn't so bad that i can't play it anymore. Overall i recomend it if you wanna' play something fun and abstract
was dope had fun until final boss. Impossible to touch after a certain amount of time fighting him. Same as that trash mantis fight don't design bosses that way sheesh
I was hyped and bought this game without watching any gameplay first. I shouldn't have done that. It is too hard to enjoy casually. Dedicated energy is needed for the game.
The gameplay is fantastic and the lore is equally amazing. Gameplay gets frustrating sometimes tho but its worth every penny.

---
Black Myth: Wukong captivated me from start to finish. It’s a game that demands patience and skill, one that can definitely stress you out with its bosses, but also rewards you with an incredible sense of satisfaction when you finally defeat them after countless attempts. That balance between challenge and reward makes it truly special.

The fact that it’s entirely in Mandarin and deeply inspired by Chinese mythology gives it a unique and beautiful identity. Everything feels authentic, magical, and culturally rich. I highly recommend this game, and I’m already looking forward to whatever Game Science creates next.

---

Black Myth: Wukong me encantó de principio a fin. Es un juego que te exige paciencia y habilidad, capaz de sacarte canas verdes con sus jefes, pero también de darte una satisfacción enorme cuando finalmente los derrotas después de tantos intentos. Esa mezcla de reto y recompensa lo hace realmente especial.

El hecho de estar en mandarín y estar profundamente inspirado en la mitología china le da una identidad única y hermosa. Todo se siente auténtico, mágico y culturalmente rico. Recomiendo este juego sin dudarlo, y ya estoy esperando con ansias lo próximo que Game Science tenga preparado.

The controls on this game are sufficiently awkward and uncomfortable as to deter me from playing. I usually do try to play a game long enough to get a handle on the controls, but this one's controls just felt terrible to me.

This is a amazing game that I highly reccommned. The best way to describe this game is that it combines dark souls like world and design with god of war like combat. The game has a lot of bosses and most of them are pretty easy or reasonable to kill with one or two bosses being the "hard" bosses for each chapter. Usually if you are stuck on a boss you can explore the chapter you are in more or explore other chapters to become stronger.  Unlike Dark Souls the game does not require to "git gud" but rather understand how to abuse the abilities you are given. You are given insane power and spells throughout the game that makes the game less about just wacking a boss and more learning to use your power.
Epic opening...can't wait to get into this game!!! Will update... Thx
combat system is satisfying and its fun to try different builds
Very fun, lots of learning when fighting every boss. Overall worth buying!
very good game and good story, player must know combo attack and grab secret place
Nice graphics and gameplay

good chapter 3 is kind of a drag with eh level design and only a few good bosses but otherwise good. the combat is a LITTLE rough and clunky for the first few chapters but ends up having a lot of depth later. graphics are awesome. chapter 6 is peak
Beautiful game, challenging gameplay, keeps one coming back for more.
Yes, Its all fun and games, until erlang and the great sages broken shell is gonna whoop ur as.

Wow. Just wow.

What a fantastic game.

Even nearly two years after launch, performance was still a little rough for me. I was getting around 70–90 FPS at max settings, native 2K, which is playable but not exactly flawless. That said, I’ve seen worse performance from far more established studios.

I don’t usually play a lot of Soulslike-style games, but this was downright fantastic. The final fight was so good that my jaw was practically on the floor. It felt cinematic, intense, mechanically satisfying, and genuinely earned.

The combat is not super deep in terms of the number of basic attacks you have, but it is very punishing when it comes to dodging, timing, and pattern recognition (in a good way). The game taught me to slow down, take the openings I was given, and stop trying to force full combos every time. Even though the base moveset is somewhat limited, landing perfect dodges and consistently pulling off “see-through” counters felt incredibly satisfying. The three different stances is where the combat truly shines though. There was something that felt natural about switching stances mid combo to fit what you need at the time. I generally biased towards thrust stance for the back step and...well the thrust to disrupt the enemy. When I was at full focus and needed some big damage, smash was ol' reliable. Or when I'm surrounded by a group of enemies I have the whirlwind from pillar, but in a boss fight, drinking your gourd from a safe position or dodging the effects of a big attack was always useful. 

The maps are visually excellent. They are mostly linear, with the occasional branching path, but the environments themselves are beautiful and memorable. Every chapter had its own distinct identity, and the game constantly looked impressive. Except for the spinning pagoda realm part in ch 3.... ♥♥♥♥ that part. 

Boss design is where the game shines the most. Some fights were annoying, but most of them felt fair once I slowed down and actually learned what the boss was doing. The best fights were genuinely peak action-game design: fast, punishing, stylish, and rewarding once everything clicked. By the end, I felt like the game had actually trained me to play better instead of just handing me more stats. Except for the Scorpion Lord from ch 4 though... ♥♥♥♥ that boss. By far the fight I had the most trouble with in the game. 

But overall, this game was incredible. The visuals, boss fights, music, atmosphere, and sense of progression all came together into something that felt special. For a studio’s first major AAA-style release, this is genuinely impressive.

Black Myth: Wukong is easily one of the best games I’ve played in a long time. I even went for the 100% completion, something I seldom do. 

I can't wait to see what Black myth game is next!
very pretty game. the parry/dodge system is really satisfying, combat overall feels great, loving the bosses so far.
I LOVE THIS GAME its really fun and the npcs put a good fight the down side is its too easy to get lost and its frustrating
The graphics and the 3d textures are insane but not only that the gameplay is really good

Yes, for the most part. It isn't as open world as I thought, which really sucks. You're walking through a small map the same as a God of War game. If that's what you like then this game is for you.

I enjoy the combat and gameplay itself though. The talent tree system is awesome, it integrates well with individual playstyles, or if you want to be OP altogether you can progress and buy everything. They did a great job writing too, although I think they played it a little too safe. I'm having difficulty progressing though and can't be bothered to get better at the game really lol. Audio cues for attacks/parries would be nice, unless there's something I'm missing (which is likely).

Also not able to run Ray Tracing above 60fps on a 5070 at 1440p. I guess I can't have everything though, I'm happy for what I do have. Thanks Game Science. Great game overall.

still on chapter 4 and already feel like i can say this confidently.

the boss fights are insane, every single one feels like its own event. the music, the cutscenes, the animations at the end of each chapter... pure art, no other way to put it. combat takes a bit to click but once it does you just cant stop.

only real issues are the map which is pretty bad and some random invisible walls that are a bit annoying. pacing between bosses can drag too.

but honestly none of that matters that much when everything else is this good.

easily one of my favorite games.
Very beautiful game, good story. Everything is perfect except for one thing. The game is too difficult and I had to constantly look up guides on how to beat bosses. It is basically a soul's game
a very long souls like game with amazing graphics

i freaking love this game i turned into a bald monk #NaMoAmitabha
jokes and laughs aside, the graphics made me come and storyline? HELLOO??? SO GOOD??
bought the game as soon as i got a gaming laptop and its my first ever game. like EVER ive only played roblox and rhythm games.
fell in love with the tiger guai ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ so hard it made me busted 10L nuhh
some cutscenes made me cry 100% no ♥♥♥♥

Great graphics. Good replayability because of build variety. Boss rush done right. It has it's issues like invisible walls and a bit of junk in the MCs moveset but holy smokes I can't stop playing it.

Great graphics. Good replayability because of build variety. Boss rush done right. It has it's issues like invisible walls and a bit of jank in the MCs moveset but holy smokes I can't stop playing it.

EDIT: I loved the Boss rush modes and there's something weirdly satisfying about playing NG+. The only gripe I have are the some few unskippable cutscenes. Still PEAK game!
insane graphics, no bug and the play is fun
Not worth the hype. But overall, the game is enjoyable.

玩的第一款动作游戏
很难想象有什么动作游戏能像这个的打斗一样精彩
great visuals, great combat, great story so far.  well worth the price!!

The devs CLEARLY dont want you to play this game more than 3 times. They know this. Also had a nice bug... when i cleared the 3rd time and skipped the credits; Im now at start menu- hit continue, but the game didnt save progress for beating the Final?!. Spawned in at stage 1 of the boss LOL no cut scene even though i had to hit the rock prompt. I wont be playing this again. NOT REPLAYBLE thus not worth the cost regardless. YES I COLLECTED ALL THE GOOD STUFF LOL Bosses get a crazy buff at 3 and 4+  making your progress meaningless. Dont like it??? Start a fresh game LMFAO If I see this dev team make another game ill probably pass. Haters moves
Best graphics until Senua's Saga, never could master the gameplay but I got far enough to really appreciate it. If it's not hard, it's usually not worth it!
a beatifull and good game we need story updates and improvements
My main complaint is with the level design being so linear, but the game is great overall.
i really like the story and the fighting mechanics and the challenge it gives even on easy difficulty.
great game! combat is amazing, the story is captivating. If you liked god of war/souls like this is it. 10 out of 10.

The most visually stunning games I’ve ever played. Every area feels handcrafted, inspired by Chinese mythology, and packed with incredible atmosphere. The boss fights are cinematic, challenging, and genuinely satisfying to overcome.

The combat feels smooth and fast, with flashy abilities and transformations that keep every fight exciting. The soundtrack, voice acting, and art direction all come together perfectly to create a memorable experience.

Easily one of the best action games in recent years. 🐒🔥

if you like dying again and again then this game is for you because Who The F*** designed these bosses, just when you think you are done with the boss fight, the boss either starts spamming one of the most ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ moves you've ever seen or they will have a 2nd Phase. The developers were probaby smoking weed when they made this game. But all jokes aside this game is truly amazing and worth the money, If you like souls-like games, then you can try this out

Excellent gameplay and storyline, unique bosses and alot to discover. Game is challenging and really tests your patience, so play with ease of mind and keep calm or else this will frustrate alot to those who cant keep up with the game's overall mechanics. Alot of customisation and great weapons and armour collection again choose them wisely or this game will punish alot. Works excellent but in some cutscens it lags with sudden fps drops only happens in cutscenes the gameplay is smooth on my configuration use nvida settings or go for lowest settings. Do not get fooled by the low settings they still preserve alot of details this games low graphics settings are very good. Overall I will give 9/10, excellent game and completely different concept.

This game was amazing. Although, I waited to play it until I upgraded my 8 year old PC. I wanted to quit many times and took a few breaks but what a rush when you finally beat an encounter you've been struggling on. I also find it interesting that many people have different views on what is hard and what is not. I am definitely playing a few story mode games after this now since I beat it. Never played souls-style games before however close it is to that, I don't know. Good story. Good gameplay.
Absolutely stunning, Great story/lore, beautiful graphics!

The best Souls-like game I have ever played in my entire life

Also runs really well
amazing game! very much beginner friendly. the mechanics and the graphics are great! would totally recommend this game to others!
A game that will push you but reward with great gameplay beautiful graphics an epic story and tons of content. This game really sets the bar. Its worth playing and worth beating at least once.
Performance is terrible. Boss design is not great and overall found it boring. Expected it to be better just a very boring action game with lame bosses
bought this game for the graphics but it had a really good story and satisfitying combat and bosses
Amazing game! Cool lore and the boss fights were cool, I've always found the monkey king lore intriguing so this mixed with intense combat make this game so much fun for me.

TLDR - B tier - 14/20
I give every game grace and give them 10/10 off rip. I use a subtraction system where you have to earn your points back. that being said read as you please. 

The devs put so much detail into the design of the game its astonishing but in this day and age nothing special. I'm glad that they put effort into properly telling the lore of all the characters as I have loved mythologies since I was a kid and love finding out anything I can. For all that they get 1 point. 11/10

My first playthrough took me 2 weeks of playing constantly when I was unemployed... 2 weeks... I understand I was trying to 100% as best as possible but that is an astonishing amount of time required to play. Especially when you consider the fact that I pride myself on speedruns. The game was perfect on my first playthrough except for the amount of time it took to complete.  10/10

My second playthrough showed me a lot of the faulty designs of the game. You're following the bath but happen to slightly misstep in course correction? cool, sit and stare for a good 3 seconds as your character needlessly does a fake falling animation. Oh it happened while you're in a fight? now it's 5 seconds. Oh you don't want to kill everything in sight because you're already level 250? too bad now the characters follow you instead of leaving after a certain distance. For all that you lose 2 points. 8/10. 

Currently fighting Erlang in my third playthrough and a problem I've had this entire playthrough is getting hit through my dodge. Instantly losing 2 points for that. Ragnarok had the same problem and people wonder why it wasn't game of the year. 6/10. Moving on I noticed that some of the bosses were starting to do new moves... On my third playthrough and you're just now doing new moves? Why not pop them when I first tried you on my first or second playthrough? 5/10. 

Moving onto the actual enemies I have lots of problems. Half the bosses in the game aren't even a boss. They're a larger version of the lesser yaogauis you already fought or they're a gimick. Don't get hit by the massive rolling ball. Dont let the rolley polleys of doom and despair hit you too much. I can count on 1 hand how many bosses I struggled with. 4/10 

The bosses that actually took some skill don't even actually have any weight to them they js have long combos. Half the boss fights in the game you spend 90% dodging and 10% actually attacking. That's not fun that's boring.  3/10 

Onto the lesser yaoguis the main problem I have is 3 lesser yaogauis you run into constantly (on their respective levels) are harder than entire final bosses. The centipede gaui gave me more problems in a 1v1 than almost any boss. That 1 lesser yaogaui is Erlang level. 2/10 The monk from the sea or whatever they're called gave me more problems than anyone in that entire level. 1/10 I feel bad so I wont even count the third one. 

Despite the fact that I have a relatively high end computer the loading screens disguised as cutscenes were so laggy I lost all care several times throughout each level. 0/10 

I love mythologies and have been studying them since I was 7. Wukong in particular has always intrigued me as being the one true mortal turned god. The story of a mere monkey wanting peace for his people and friends. A monkey forced to watch the love of his life, his best friend, and even his own home get burned by the gods. Because of my love for wukong the game earns back 4 points. 4/10. 

The story telling, despite being somewhat faulty because of the size of the game, is immaculate. I'm enthralled with the cutscenes after every level. Beautiful animation and stories that brought back memories long lost. They didn't just do the characters justice they treated them as they legends they are. 5/10

the fact the devs even hinted to other stories being eventually told like lu bu and lady 9 tails makes me beyond excited. For that I feel I will be coming back eventually to raise the grade. for putting that idea, that hope, in me you get 2 points. 7/10 

Ill be using the french grading system instead of the american grading system because america demands perfection while being a third world country with a gucci belt on. 

7/10 = 14/20 
B tier game

I bought this game some years ago and never played it until recently. I love the game and how beautifully it was designed. However, it's not as fun the second time beating the game. I don't think it's worth playing for a second time. I wish they offered more than the same thing and no additional rewards. Once you beat the game don't play it again. Everything is super easy after that. I was actually mad at how easy Tiger Vanguard was the second time around. This boss almost made me quit playing the game the first time! And there's a lot of open space that I feel they should've had more treasures to find. I honestly love this game and this is my opinion of it that's all.
I played this game for 34hrs and I am just on chapter 2......love everything about the game minus the fact that I am clearly very sucky at soul like games ;__;
one of the most beautiful games i have ever played. extremely fun and worth the mon(k)ey.

I have played through the Entire Game! 204hrs on record! Very happy with it! Kinda checks all boxes! Good Story✓ Good Combat✓ Side Quests✓ Everything you want in an action combat open world this game has it! What I hope for in the future is more games like this! Long enough to enjoy!!

Really fun and challenging game. Storyline is great too. Nothing negative I can think of other than the fact (not really negative anyways) that you need the most recent generation of graphics card and processor. If not, you'll be stuck at the Tornado boss with like 40 frames per second

If you love fast-paced combat and Action RPGs, this game is a must-play. The visuals are breathtaking, and the gameplay is just as incredible.

8.5/10
Fun game but the first skill check is right at the 2 hr mark but if you upgrade your character it gets easier and easier

Monkey hat, monkey hat
Who de dibble knitted dat?
If you die I hope dat I
Inherit de dam monkey hat.
- A Rastifarian Poem

This game is great, it's hard, the art is goregous and best of all it makes you think and learn. If you've played the souls games or the God of War series this game is like a happy middle between the two. The animated short stories that play at the end of each chapter are beutifully handcrafted and some of the most memorable parts of the game. The combat is pretty diverse, with spells, transformations, wepon & armour customisation and Qi just to name a few mechanics that go into it. The amount of content and depth of even the minor systems like crafting and medicine is absoletly insaine but not so crazy that it feels overwhelming.

Some gripes, Chapter 6 really fumbles the bag, the input reading that is present in some boss fights goes from minor inconvenience to painfully obvious. This chapter being the last and worst chapter does suck because the rest of this game is legit perfect. The pacing of the story is a little rough too, chapter 3 is just too godamn long and the end after the miserable first 3/4 of chapter 6 is pretty abrupt and thematically unclear only because of the misery and lack of symbolism in most of chapter 6. 
Despite that the final fight (litterly fighting yourself) is wounderful (despite input reading ahh) and challenging and effectively concludes the game and gets you to understand the narrative. NAratively this game is about internal struggle and internal mastery, sometimes this theme is lost during the easter egg hunt for parts of your own soul but the ending does tie it together nicely. The feeling of getting better, mastering the mechanics and suceeding follows the same path wukong does during the story and thats truely special. 

Overall 67/10, theres a lot of lore and story to keep you engauged even after you beat the game, and you can't do everything on a first run through. For $90AUD you're actually getting your moneys worth so buy it, idc who you are play it, you'll really feel a sense of growth.

TLDR: Buddist Monkey game is straight fire...
Incredibly great story although the final chapter was a little hard to navigate but the rest was fantastic. 10/10 would highly recommend this game.
it is an amazing game. i definitely recommend this game. however i get that it is a bit pricey. wait for lunar new year for 25% off. XD

The game is quite unique , i mean its difficult at first to defeat the characters and the side characters, eventually after fighting them off and dying for quite a few times you suddenly notice those patterns with every fight which you use to defeat them and it becomes easier to defeat them than the first time, a bit complex with all those thing related to spells , spirits and skills to upgrade.

This game is overrated as ♥♥♥♥!! i will say the graphics and cutscenes are top notch but the areas are really bland and the combat gets way too repetitive.

Gameplay is enjoyable with some beautiful scenery/environments. This dark souls style game has a good level of challenge without being too punishing. Unlike souls games when you die you don't lose experience/currency which makes death less aggravating. The story is interesting although I am not very familiar with the folklore behind Wukong so some of the references were lost on me. That is no fault of the developer though, just an observation for those who are not familiar on Chinese folklore. Definitely felt like I got a good amount of gameplay for the money with taking about 75 hours from start to finish. Could certainly be done quicker but I explored every chapter thoroughly which takes some time. Overall a very enjoyable game with a lot of replayability with the NG+ option
This game is very rewarding if your a completionest who does NPC interactions, exploration, enjoys very hard bosses, finding secrets etc. This game is perfect for you.
great game u need good good graphics card to fully appreciate tho
The graphics are nice and the combat is fun.
Some boss fights are hecticcc, but what a gameee.

honestly feels like one of the few modern action RPGs that actually delivers on its hype.

The boss fights are incredible, the visuals are insane, and the Chinese mythology setting makes the whole experience feel fresh compared to typical fantasy games. Combat feels smooth and satisfying once you fully understand the dodge/timing mechanics.

At its core, combat focuses on performing combo attacks on enemies and dodging their attacks, though you can get tons of cool abilities that change up how you fight! I think there are over 100 unique bosses as well, which is insane, and almost all of them are fun! A few are super hard as well! 10/10 chinese mythology atmosphere with cool and very mysterious stories you gotta piece together along the way!

Super fun game that I really enjoyed the story and difficulty of. Only frustrating thing is that to 100% you need to get a bunch of very specific rare pieces of equipment so I stopped after a few ng+.

relentless abuse without a break is exactly what i want after working hard all day. great design both visually and from a narrative point of view, but this game spikes my cortisol so bad i might have a brain aneurysm before getting close to finishing it.
one of the better game out there, rough launch but smooth sail no complain would love for a DLC or sequel

This was my first dip into the "git gud" types of games, and it was overall a positive experience for me, enough for me to bother getting all achievements. I'd rate it around 8.5/10 for my time spent. I'll go into some detail below: 

In terms of story, I definitely felt it was very scattered, even though I'm familiar with the source material. Normally this would be a big turn-off, as I really enjoy story-driven games, but once I accepted that the game is mostly focused on gameplay, I stopped being bothered by the narrative. As far as I'm concerned, it's like a classic Mario game where you just go through some worlds, beat the final boss, and be done with it. 

As for the gameplay, I was happy with the relatively simple set of options where I could focus on the fundamentals of dodge/counter/attack without feeling overwhelmed by complex combos and maneuvers. While this is my first entry into "difficult" games, I have played plenty of action/adventure type games like Assassin's Creed, Witcher 3, and God of War, Horizon, and so on, and I've always been the type to keep things simple. I dodge, I parry, I attack. I primarily use basic attacks and don't like touching skills. I do not need to be flashy to dominate my enemies. 

My main complaint comes with some bosses/enemies whose hit(hurt?)boxes need tuning. These are normally large enemies where the lock-on mechanism targets the feet, and it's just way too easy to miss attacks due to the main character being pushed away by collision with the rest of the enemy's body. I'll swallow the "git gud" of missing because the limb moves, but it always feels horrible to miss a 4-point focus attack because the staff lands just short of the enemy's paw while the enemy is staying still. 

I also have issues with chapter 3, which dragged on and on and on, especially the Mani Wheel prison section where checkpoints are pretty sparse, and where you can so easily fall to your doom. Thunderclap Temple was also very annoying to explore because of those lightning mage guys placed everywhere...all with just two checkpoints at the entrance and end. I think chapter 4 hit the right balance of exploration versus length. 

As far as difficulty goes, I'm probably not the right person to ask. Although I'm new to these kinds of games, I've always played games at the highest difficulty, even for the first playthrough. I also tend to do self-imposed challenges for these games if I find the gameplay fun enough to practice further. Because of this, I was rather dissatisfied with the lack of difficulty options for this game, since I knew it would be balanced relative to the "average" player. I would not call my first playthrough experience a struggle by any means, including the secret and final bosses. I was at least happy with the Gauntlet and Return of Rivals so that I could fight any boss I wanted at max difficulty without having to do too many NG+ cycles (I don't like replaying games). Even then I had to purposely disable my res skill and limit gourd uses because the bosses were too easy otherwise. 

No comment on graphics. Optimizations aside, it's mostly a question of how much money can you throw at your rig.
this game its very realistic and hard find bosses get powers find items and more

Good game about being a monkey. Thats all you really need to understand.

Still different enough to be enjoyable and unique but I've begun to worry that this is a flash in the pan with these types as there isn't enough innovation going on. Hopefully that ages poorly.

Good game up until chapter 6 (last) and then it seems like the devs just gave up and released the game. Bosses aren't too difficult. i'm not sure how nerfed they are since release, but only a few required more than 5 attempts and most are one shot bosses if you just know how to press dodge. If you're looking for more of a challange, don't put skill points in A Pluck of Many or don't use it.

The gearing and weapons system is weird as the game at this stage isn't difficult enough to require so much variety. Unless it's needed in NG+ they could have used the time designing all that on chapter 6 instead.

这游戏是真的离谱，难度直接拉满，打得我人都麻了。
本来只是想当个猴子西天取经，结果一路被教育做人（不对，是做猴）。

打着打着突然有种既视感：从东到西一路漂泊，风吹日晒，吃尽苦头，仿佛在和各种“强敌”周旋。
只能说，懂的都懂，不懂的也别问。🟡🔵🔴

西行确实是往西走，这点一点没骗人。

总结：痛苦拉满，快乐也拉满，属于是边骂边打，边死边上头。
猴哥牛逼，西行万岁。
